content.heading: Nature and Purpose of This Archive
content.text: VeteransLedger is a personal historical archive dedicated to the documentation and scholarly study of the Second World War (1939–1945) and its political context (1919–1945). All content — including military records, personnel profiles, correspondence, armament specifications, campaign histories, and political documentation — is presented strictly for educational, research, and historical understanding.

content.heading: No Endorsement of Documented Ideologies
content.text: The reproduction, translation, quotation, or description of speeches, symbols, propaganda, ideology, insignia, or the actions and policies of the National Socialist German Workers' Party (NSDAP), the Axis powers, or any associated organisation, regime, or individual does not constitute endorsement, support, glorification, or promotion of those ideologies, policies, or actions. VeteransLedger unequivocally and without reservation condemns fascism, National Socialism, antisemitism, racial supremacism, genocide, and all related ideologies and the violence and atrocities they produced.

content.heading: Historical Documentation Requirement
content.text: Understanding the history of the Second World War — including its perpetrators, their methods, their beliefs, and their military operations — is essential to honest historical memory, to anti-fascist education, and to the prevention of future atrocities. The suppression of historical documentation serves neither scholarship nor justice. This archive presents the historical record as it was, not as it should have been.

content.heading: Historical Accuracy
content.text: While we strive for historical accuracy in all content, this archive is compiled by a private researcher and is not a peer-reviewed academic publication. Sources used include secondary scholarship, official records, military histories, and primary documents. Casualty figures, dates, unit strengths, and operational details are often subject to ongoing revision in historical scholarship; where significant disagreement exists among historians, we have attempted to note the range of estimates. Do not rely solely on this archive for academic citation — always consult primary sources, institutional archives, and peer-reviewed scholarship.

content.heading: Sensitive and Distressing Content
content.text: This archive documents warfare, military operations, political violence, atrocities, and significant loss of life. Some material — including letters from soldiers at the front, records of casualties, and documentation of war crimes — may be distressing to readers. We present this material because honest historical memory requires confronting difficult truths. We encourage readers who find the content distressing to take breaks and to seek support if needed.

content.heading: Personnel Records and Living Individuals
content.text: All individuals documented on this site served in or were associated with the Axis armed forces or political structures during the period 1919–1945. We make every effort to ensure that records relate to deceased individuals. If you are a family member concerned about a living relative's information appearing on this site, please contact us immediately via the contact form and we will address the matter promptly.

content.heading: Letters and Personal Correspondence
content.text: Personal letters preserved in this archive are historical documents. They reflect the perspectives, beliefs, and circumstances of their authors writing under wartime conditions. Inclusion of a letter in this archive does not constitute endorsement of the views expressed by the author. These letters are preserved as primary historical sources that offer insight into the human experience of war.

content.heading: Jurisdiction and Legal Compliance
content.text: The display of historical materials — including symbols, text, and imagery associated with the NSDAP and Axis powers — is subject to legal restrictions in some jurisdictions. This archive is operated for educational purposes; where national laws restrict the reproduction of certain historical materials, access from those jurisdictions may be limited. Users are responsible for ensuring their access to and use of this site complies with the laws of their jurisdiction.

content.heading: Advertising Content
content.text: This site may display contextual advertisements served by Google AdSense or similar third-party advertising platforms. Advertisement content is determined automatically by the advertising platform. VeteransLedger does not pre-screen individual advertisements and does not endorse the products or services advertised. If you encounter an advertisement that appears inappropriate or inconsistent with the educational purpose of this archive, please report it using the contact form.

content.heading: No Professional Advice
content.text: Nothing on this site constitutes legal, academic, military, or professional advice of any kind. Historical information is provided for general educational purposes. For expert analysis, consult qualified historians, academics, and primary source archives.

content.heading: Limitation of Liability
content.text: VeteransLedger is provided 'as is' and makes no warranties, expressed or implied, regarding the completeness, accuracy, reliability, or suitability of the information contained herein. To the fullest extent permitted by law, VeteransLedger accepts no liability for any direct, indirect, incidental, or consequential damages arising from your use of or reliance upon this archive.

content.heading: Corrections and Feedback
content.text: We welcome corrections, additions, and scholarly feedback. If you identify a factual error, an inaccurate translation, or a misattributed source, please contact us via the contact form. Verified corrections will be implemented as promptly as practicable.
